title: Decalepis nervosa
taxonomy: Decalepis nervosa is a genus of flowering plants in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the order Gentianales and is native to the Western Ghats of India. The classification of Decalepis nervosa is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids. The plant is further classified into the family Apocynaceae and the genus Decalepis. Its unique classification places it among the diverse and complex family of Apocynaceae.
uses: Decalepis nervosa has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its medicinal properties are also attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Additionally, research has shown that the plant may have potential in the treatment of diabetes and obesity due to its antidiabetic and anti-obesity effects. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential candidate for the development of new medicines. Further studies are needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Decalepis nervosa.

side_effects: Decalepis nervosa is reported to cause gastrointestinal side effects in some individuals, including nausea and diarrhea. intro: Decalepis nervosa is a perennial climber native to the Western Ghats of India, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-diabetic properties, which may aid in the management of various diseases such as diabetes, cancer, and cardiovascular disorders.
